content.text: The Service only has the `gcs` port (6379). I'll patch it to add the `dashboard` port (8265).

input.description: Add dashboard port 8265 to ray-head Service

input.command: kubectl -n ray exec curl-test -- curl -sS -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://ray-head:8265/api/cluster_status

content.thinking:
The ray-head pod is only listening on port 6379 (GCS), not 8265 (dashboard). This suggests the Ray dashboard isn't started or is bound to a different port.

Let me check the Ray configuration in the pod - maybe the dashboard is disabled or bound to a different port.

content.text: The pod is only listening on 6379. The Ray dashboard isn't running inside the container. Let me check the ray-head configuration.

content.thinking:
Now I see the full picture:

1. The ray-head container starts Ray with `--include-dashboard=false`, which means no dashboard HTTP listener is created.
2. However, the dashboard process IS actually running at PID 153: `/home/ray/anaconda3/bin/python ... ray/dashboard/dashboard.py --host=127.0.0.1 --port=8265 ...`
3. It's bound to `127.0.0.1:8265`, not `0.0.0.0:8265` - that's why the `/proc/net/tcp` check didn't show it (it checks for 0.0.0.0 listens differently from loopback).
4. The `--include-dashboard=false` flag should prevent the dashboard from starting, but it's running anyway - this might be a quirk of the Ray version being used.
5. Since the dashboard is only listening on loopback, the Service can route traffic to port 8265 internally, but the pod isn't accepting external connections on that port.

The fix requires two changes: enable the dashboard explicitly with `--include-dashboard=true` and bind it to `0.0.0.0` instead of `127.0.0.1` so it's accessible from outside the pod. `--include-dashboard=false` doesn't actually disable the dashboard in Ray 2.9.0—it just binds it to `127.0.0.1` instead of the node IP. So when the Service tries to route traffic to the pod's actual IP address, the connection fails because the dashboard is only listening on loopback. I need to either remove the flag entirely or set it to `true` so the dashboard binds to `0.0.0.0` and becomes accessible from outside the pod.

The fix is to update the Deployment to remove `--include-dashboard=false`, add `--dashboard-host=0.0.0.0` to explicitly bind to all interfaces, and expose port 8265 in the container spec. This will force a pod restart with the dashboard properly accessible.

content.text: The dashboard process is running but bound to `127.0.0.1:8265` (loopback only) because of `--include-dashboard=false`. I need to update the Deployment command to enable the dashboard and bind it to `0.0.0.0`.

input.description: Update ray-head Deployment to enable dashboard on 0.0.0.0:8265
